title: Hugging Face and SkyPilot add zero-egress storage for portable AI workloads
friendlyTitle: Hugging Face and SkyPilot make AI jobs easier to move between clouds
summary: Hugging Face and SkyPilot now let AI jobs mount Hugging Face Buckets, model repos, and dataset repos through an hf:// path while running compute across clouds, Kubernetes, Slurm, or on-prem systems. Hugging Face says the storage path has no egress fees, so workloads can move to available GPUs without copying data between cloud buckets.
friendlySummary: SkyPilot jobs can now read models and datasets directly from Hugging Face storage, even when the compute runs on another cloud or cluster.
whyItMatters: GPU availability and data-location costs are daily constraints for AI teams. Portable storage plus portable compute can lower friction for training, fine-tuning, and serving models where capacity is cheapest or available fastest.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This can save teams time and money. Instead of copying huge AI files around, they can run work where GPUs are available and keep the data in one place.
